claim: Session 21 viability assessment for the toxicity-prediction research direction. Three viability gates evaluated. Gate A (simulator architecture supports additive inhibition layer): PASS — cell_sim/layer3_reactions/reversible.py computes catalysis rates as kcat * mm_saturation_factor, providing a clean injection point for competitive / non-competitive / uncompetitive K_i terms that reduce to identity at zero inhibitor concentration. Gate B (compound-target data coverage): PROVISIONAL_PASS pending Session 22 Colab measurement — every relevant database (ChEMBL, BindingDB, PubChem, DrugBank, OpenFDA) returns HTTP 403 from the Claude Code sandbox; only raw.githubusercontent.com is reachable, so all compound-target curation must happen Colab-side. Coverage estimate from literature: of the 155 Syn3A SBML gene-associated reactions, ~30-40 enzymes are conserved bacterial drug targets that should have hundreds-to-thousands of ChEMBL inhibitor entries each, but the locus_tag -> UniProt -> ChEMBL_target join needs explicit measurement. Gate C (validation dataset >= 30 Mycoplasma compound-MIC pairs): PROVISIONAL_PASS — Mycoplasma is well-studied; conservative literature estimate is 50-80 antibiotics with published MIC against M. pneumoniae or M. genitalium, comfortably above the spec's 30-compound minimum but below the 100-compound 'strong result' floor. Beta-lactams and glycopeptides MUST be excluded from the validation set because Mycoplasma intrinsically rejects them (no cell wall) — including them would inflate true-negatives artificially. Decision: provisional GO with a Gate B measurement gate before any implementation. If Gate B comes back below 10 Syn3A enzymes with traceable inhibitor data, the project does not proceed and the negative finding is itself the deliverable.
source_detail: Reproducible by reading cell_sim/layer3_reactions/reversible.py (lines 48-72 + 132-180) for Gate A architecture; running scripts/bench_rust_vs_python.py-style network probes for sandbox reachability tests; SBML inventory via parse_sbml on Syn3A_updated.xml. Full assessment at memory_bank/concepts/toxicity/VIABILITY.md.

gate_a_architecture.regression_invariant: at [I]=0 the new code path must produce identical event sequences to the current version; verifiable by replaying v15 sweep + asserting bit-identical confusion matrix (tp=287 fp=3 tn=69 fn=96)

gate_c_validation_set.spec_glass_2017_reference_status: LIKELY_TYPO — Glass 2006 PNAS exists; no Glass 2017 minimal-cell paper located. User should verify before relying on it.

structural_caveats.beta_lactams_and_glycopeptides: MUST EXCLUDE from validation set — Mycoplasma has no cell wall, so these are intrinsically inactive; including them would inflate true-negatives without testing the simulator's mechanism predictions
structural_caveats.membrane_disruptors: polymyxin-class compounds bypass the metabolic network; cannot be mechanistically predicted without a membrane-biophysics model — flag as known-failure-mode category
structural_caveats.cross_organism_ki_transfer: published K_i typically measured on E. coli or human enzymes, not Mycoplasma orthologs; will be a source of error in MCC
structural_caveats.mic_unit_conversion: MICs are ug/mL; simulator uses mM; need a single canonical conversion path with unit tests

sessions_ahead_if_gate_b_passes.session_22: Gate B measurement (Colab notebook): syn3a_enzyme_inhibitor_coverage.csv + headline number
sessions_ahead_if_gate_b_passes.session_23: Validation set assembly (Colab): Mycoplasma MIC table for >= 30 compounds with cited sources
sessions_ahead_if_gate_b_passes.session_24: cell_sim/layer3_reactions/inhibition.py + zero-inhibitor regression test that v15 sweep reproduces bit-identical
sessions_ahead_if_gate_b_passes.session_25: Toxicity readout layer in Layer 6 + first end-to-end prediction
sessions_ahead_if_gate_b_passes.session_26: Run validation set; compute MCC + mechanism-agreement scores

caveats: This is an ASSESSMENT, not a measurement of toxicity-prediction accuracy. No simulator code was modified. No compound-target data was fetched. The headline numbers (30-40 conserved drug-target enzymes, 50-80 Mycoplasma-active antibiotics) come from literature familiarity, not from a database query — Session 22's Colab notebook turns these estimates into measurements.
Sandbox reachability was probed at one point in time (April 2026). If the proxy configuration changes the situation could improve, but the standing assumption is that Colab-side curation is required.

The spec's reference to 'Glass 2017' could not be located by the assessor. Glass et al. 2006 PNAS (the M. genitalium minimal-genome paper) and the JCVI Syn3.0 papers (Hutchison 2016, Breuer 2019) are real and accessible; a 2017 follow-up specifically on compound screening of minimal cells is not. Flag for the user to verify before relying on it as a validation source.

Three viability gates is the minimum framing. There are additional risks (kinetic-parameter cross-organism transfer, MIC unit conversion, toxicity-threshold circular tuning) that are real but not blocking — they're documented in VIABILITY.md as risks to manage during implementation, not viability gates.

If Gate B (Session 22 Colab measurement) returns < 10 Syn3A enzymes with usable inhibitor data, the project halts. That negative result is itself the contribution — 'mechanism-aware toxicity prediction is not viable for Syn3A given current ChEMBL coverage of conserved Mycoplasma enzyme orthologs.' Document and stop.

The 'minimum publishable result' floor (MCC >= 0.40 with >= 30 compounds, plus 3 mechanism-agreement case studies) is the appropriate target for a part-time 3-4 month student project. The 'strong result' floor (MCC >= 0.55 with >= 100 compounds + Pearson r >= 0.5 with MIC) is closer to a multi-month full-time effort and should not be the v1 goal.
